critic_raw:
**Issue 1: Structured data preprocessing fit on full dataset before splitting.** Section 3 states: "All preprocessing transformers are designed for stability in parameter estimation by being fit on the entire data prior to splitting" — specifically for structured features (age, sex, ejection fraction, BNP, comorbidities, medications). This explicitly leaks test-set statistics (means, standard deviations for continuous features; category frequencies for one-hot encoding) into training normalization. The temporal split is correct, but the preprocessing leakage partially undermines its integrity.

**Issue 2: The baseline comparison does not isolate multi-modal contribution.** The baseline is a unimodal model using clinical notes only (frozen BioBERT + BiLSTM). The proposed multi-modal model adds echocardiogram images and structured data. This means the comparison conflates two architectural differences: (1) the addition of imaging and structured modalities, and (2) the addition of a structured-data branch. A proper ablation would require at minimum a text+structured model and a text+image model to isolate each modality's contribution. The experiment cannot determine whether imaging specifically adds value or whether structured data (which is often highly predictive for heart failure) drives the improvement.

**Issue 3: DenseNet-121 pretrained on ImageNet applied to echocardiograms without domain-adapted fine-tuning.** Section 3 uses frozen DenseNet-121 (ImageNet weights) for echocardiogram feature extraction, explicitly stating no label-driven fine-tuning is performed. Echocardiograms are grayscale or pseudo-color ultrasound images with spatial frequency content and texture statistics fundamentally different from ImageNet photographs. Frozen ImageNet features applied to echocardiograms have been shown in the literature to perform poorly unless domain adaptation is used. The design conflates "no fine-tuning on labels" (preventing label leakage) with "frozen ImageNet weights are an adequate feature extractor for ultrasound" — the second claim is not supported and is the most likely cause of imaging modality underperformance.
all_issues_raised: Structured feature preprocessing transformers (normalization statistics for continuous features, one-hot encoding vocabularies) are fit on the full dataset before the temporal split, leaking test-set distribution information into training normalization for structured inputs.

The baseline is text-only while the proposed model adds both imaging and structured data simultaneously; the comparison cannot distinguish whether imaging, structured data, or their combination drives any performance gain, making the hypothesis about multi-modal integration untestable.

Frozen DenseNet-121 (ImageNet-pretrained) is used as the echocardiogram feature extractor with no domain adaptation; ImageNet features are well-documented to be poor representations for ultrasound images, and if echocardiogram embeddings provide near-random signal, the 'multi-modal' advantage is entirely driven by the structured data branch.
all_issues_adjudicated: Structured feature preprocessing transformers (normalization statistics for continuous features, one-hot encoding vocabularies) are fit on the full dataset before the temporal split, leaking test-set distribution information into training normalization for structured inputs.

The baseline is text-only while the proposed model adds both imaging and structured data simultaneously; the comparison cannot distinguish whether imaging, structured data, or their combination drives any performance gain, making the hypothesis about multi-modal integration untestable.

Frozen DenseNet-121 (ImageNet-pretrained) is used as the echocardiogram feature extractor with no domain adaptation; ImageNet features are well-documented to be poor representations for ultrasound images, and if echocardiogram embeddings provide near-random signal, the 'multi-modal' advantage is entirely driven by the structured data branch.
